revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[de:Gyōgan-ji (Kyōto)]][[en:Gyōgan-ji]][[ja:行願寺]][[zh:行願寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Koudou-F3028.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Nakagyō Ward|qid=Q1075455|1=Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.0163|N|135.767761|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|qid=Q750721|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}}} {{Otheruses|Gyōgan-ji Temple in Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Jōfukuin Temple in Kōya, Wakayama Prefecture|Jōfukuin (Kōya, Wakayama)}} {{Japanese temple |name = Gyōgan-ji |image = [[File:koudou-F3028.JPG|300px|Gyōgan-ji Kōdō]] |caption = Main Hall |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1075455|lt=Nakagyō-ku|1=Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto}} Teramachi-dōri Takeyachō Agaru Gyōganji Monzenchō 17 |position = {{Coord|35|0|58.68|N|135|46|3.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |map2 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=35|lat_min=0|lat_sec=58.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=3.94|label=Gyōgan-ji|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=pp|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |mountain = Reiyūzan (霊麀山) |sect = {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai sect|1=Tiantai}} |temple Rank = |main image = {{ill|Thousand-armed Kannon|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|ja|千手観音|1=Thousand-armed Kannon|2=ja|3=千手観音|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|4=fr|5=Thousand-armed Kannon|6=ko|7=관세음보살|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thousand-armed Kannon|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |established = 1004 (Kankō 1) |founder = Gyōen |official name = Reiyūzan Gyōgan-ji |alternate names = Kōdō (革堂) |pilgrimage sites = {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|qid=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}} #19
{{ill|Rakuyō Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Kannon Pilgrimage|1=33 Sacred Sites of Luoyang Kannon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10380674}} #4
{{ill|Miyako Shichifukujin|ja|都七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako Shichifukujin|1=Miyako Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10560192}} (Jurojin)
{{ill|Sacred Sites of Kamigata|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of Kamigata|qq=}} #pp4 (Kyoto #34) |cultural properties = Main Hall, Bell Tower (Kyoto City-designated Tangible Cultural Properties) }} '''Gyōgan-ji''' (行願寺), commonly known as '''Kōdō''' (革堂), is a {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai sect|1=Tiantai}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1075455|lt=Nakagyō-ku|1=Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. The temple’s mountain name is Reiyūzan (霊麀山), and its principal image is the {{ill|Thousand-armed Kannon|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|ja|千手観音|1=Thousand-armed Kannon|2=ja|3=千手観音|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|4=fr|5=Thousand-armed Kannon|6=ko|7=관세음보살|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thousand-armed Kannon|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is the 19th temple of the {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|qid=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}, and the only nunnery along the pilgrimage route. The mantra of the principal image: "On bazara tarama kiriku sowaka." The temple’s '''Goeika''' (pilgrimage hymn): "Viewing flowers, I now find all my desires fulfilled; the myriad grasses in the courtyard are at their peak." == History == According to the {{ill|Hyakuren-shō|ja|百錬抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuren-shō|1=Hyakurenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580870}} and {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|qid=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}, Gyōgan-ji was established in 1004 (Kankō 1) by Gyōen, who restored the North Edge Hall (Ichijō Hokuhendō) at the site of Ichijō’s northern boundary to create the temple. Records from 989 (Eiso 1) indicate the North Edge Hall already existed before Gyōgan-ji’s establishment. Gyōen was formerly a hunter who experienced an awakening when he shot a pregnant deer and saw its fawn born from the dead mother. Regretting his actions, he entered the Buddhist path, wearing the deer’s skin as a reminder of his past. This earned him the nickname '''Kawa Hijiri''' ("The Skin Saint") and gave the temple its colloquial name, '''Kōdō''' ("The Hall of Leather"). He is believed to have hailed from Yokokawa on {{ill|Mount Hiei|en|Mount Hiei|qid=Q748040|lt=Mount Hiei|1=Mount Hiei}}. Notably, {{ill|Fujiwara no Akihito|ja|藤原顕信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Akihito|1=Fujiwara no Akinobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623643}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|qid=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}, was tonsured by Gyōen in 1012. Though reconstructed multiple times after lightning strikes (pp40) and fires (pp51, 1242), the temple was relocated twice due to urban development. Initially moved to Teramachi Kōjindō by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} in 1590 (Tenshō 18), it was relocated to its current location in 1708 following the {{ill|Hōei Fire|ja|宝永の大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei Fire|1=Great Fire of Hōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452579}}. Today, Gyōgan-ji is also known as the "Cat Temple," for providing shelter to stray cats. Proceeds from temple goods and donations support their care. == Ghost Ema Legend == Gyōgan-ji is home to a legendary ema (votive tablet) depicting a tragic story. During the late Edo period, a maid named Obun was scolded by her employer, a devout Nichiren follower, for singing a Kannon hymn while caring for a child. She died as a result of severe punishment. Her ghost appeared at Gyōgan-ji’s vigil, revealing the truth. The ema includes a mirror that belonged to her. == Grounds == [[File:Gyôgan-ji Temple Kôdô - Jurôjin-Shindô.jpg|thumb|Jurojin Shrine]] [[File:Gyôgan-ji Temple Kôdô - Jimusho.jpg|thumb|Temple Office]] [[File:Gyôgan-ji Temple Kôdô - Seven Lucky Gods.jpg|thumb|Seven Lucky Gods Statues]] * '''Main Hall''' (Kyoto City-designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Reconstructed in 1815 (Bunka 12). * Gyōgan-ji Hall - Includes a treasure hall on the second floor. * '''Kuri''' (temple kitchen) * '''Aizen-dō''' * '''Jurojin Shrine''' - Dedicated to Jurojin, established during the Azuchi-Momoyama period. * '''Seven Lucky Gods Statues''' * '''Chintaku Reifu Shrine''' - Dedicated to Chintaku Reifu-shin (Polaris/Myoken Bosatsu). * '''Bell Tower''' (Kyoto City-designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Reconstructed in 1804 (Bunka 1). * '''Kamo Daimyōjin Gorintō''' - A large five-element pagoda dedicated to Kamo Daimyōjin. * '''Sanmon''' (main gate) == Cultural Properties == ; Kyoto City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties * Main Hall * Bell Tower == Pilgrimage Sites == ; {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|qid=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 18 {{ill|Rokkaku-dō|en|Rokkaku-dō|qid=Q599378|lt=Rokkaku-dō|1=Rokkaku-dō}} * '''19 Gyōgan-ji (Kōdō)''' - 20 {{ill|Yoshimine-dera|en|Yoshimine-dera|qid=Q169621|lt=Yoshimine-dera|1=Yoshimine-dera}} ; {{ill|Rakuyō Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Kannon Pilgrimage|1=33 Sacred Sites of Luoyang Kannon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10380674}} : 3 {{ill|Gojo-in|ja|護浄院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojo-in|1=Gojo-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632524}} * '''4 Gyōgan-ji (Kōdō)''' - 5 {{ill|Shinshōgokuraku-ji|en|Shinshōgokuraku-ji|qid=Q1089231|lt=Shinnyo-do|1=Shinshōgokuraku-ji}} ; {{ill|Miyako Shichifukujin|ja|都七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako Shichifukujin|1=Miyako Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10560192}} (Jurojin) ; {{ill|Sacred Sites of Kamigata|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of Kamigata|qq=}} : pp3 {{ill|Heian Shrine|en|Heian Shrine|qid=Q385141|lt=Heian Shrine|1=Heian Shrine}} * '''pp4 Gyōgan-ji (Kōdō)''' - pp5 {{ill|Shōren-in|en|Shōren-in|qid=Q377386|lt=Shoren-in|1=Shōren-in}} == Access == * 7-minute walk from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|qid=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station (Kyoto)|1=Marutamachi Station}} on the Kyoto Municipal Subway Karasuma Line. * 6-minute walk from {{ill|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|en|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|qid=Q2244767|lt=Jingū-Marutamachi Station|1=Jingū-Marutamachi Station}} on the Keihan Ōtō Line. == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Shōwa Kyoto Meisho Zue, Volume 5 - Rakuchū|author=竹村俊則|publisher=Shinshindō|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei - Kyoto|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == * [https://kaudau.jp/ Official Site of Gyōgan-ji] * [https://saikoku33.gr.jp/place/19 Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage #19 Gyōgan-ji] * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?category_id=7&tourism_id=263 Kyoto Tourism Navi: Gyōgan-ji] {{Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|行願寺|version=104352544|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[de:Gyōgan-ji (Kyōto)]][[zh:行願寺]] }} [[ja:行願寺]] {{Commons category|Gyōgan-ji}} {{Commons category|Gyōgan-ji}} {{Commons category|Gyōgan-ji}} {{wikidata link|Q10919351}} [[Category:1004 establishments]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buddhistischer Wallfahrtsort]] [[Category:Mahayana]] [[Category:Saigoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Temples in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Temples of Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:Thirty-Three Kannon Pilgrimage Sites of Rakuyo]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
